ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002400 represents chocolate cake as a food product category; used as closest parent as no specific multi-layer chocolate mousse ganache cake entry exists.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers chocolate cake as a category; used as closest parent since no specific triple-layer mousse-ganache variant exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for chocolate mousse ganache layer cake; SNOMED CT does not provide sufficient granularity for this dessert subtype.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard slice of approximately 120 g, representing a typical single-serve portion of a layered chocolate mousse and ganache cake as served at a premium café in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Chocolate cake with chocolate frosting, commercially prepared — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 18147)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Chococapy Wonderland is a proprietary Singapore café dessert (Kazo Premium, Funan) comprising layers of chocolate mousse, moist chocolate sponge, and chocolate ganache. No exact entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN for this specific product. USDA SR Legacy NDB 18147 (chocolate cake with chocolate frosting, commercially prepared) is used as the closest defensible analogue for a rich, multi-layer chocolate cake. Values have been reviewed for plausibility against the product description. The mousse and ganache layers increase fat and sugar content relative to a plain chocolate sponge; values are consistent with the USDA analogue and are treated as a reasonable estimate. Energy recalculated using Atwater factors as a cross-check.

gi_source.title: Chocolate cake, made from packet mix with frosting — Glycemic Index entry, University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: The University of Sydney GI Database reports a GI of approximately 38 (low) for chocolate cake made from packet mix with frosting (Foster-Powell et al., Am J Clin Nutr 2002; 76:5–56). Chococapy Wonderland is a rich layered chocolate mousse and ganache cake; its high fat and moderate fibre content would suppress glycemic response similarly to or below the analogue. GI of 38 is used as a conservative analogous estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (38 × 62.4) / 100 ≈ 23.7, where carbohydrate per 120 g serving = 52.0 × 1.2 = 62.4 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 299 mg per 100 g; a 120 g serving contributes approximately 359 mg sodium, representing about 16% of the 2,300 mg daily limit recommended by the American Heart Association.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should limit portion size to a small slice and account for the sodium contribution within their daily dietary sodium budget, particularly if other high-sodium foods are consumed in the same day.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (34 g per 100 g) and high glycemic load per serving (approximately 23.7), despite a low GI of 38, due to the large carbohydrate load in a standard serving.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this dessert as an occasional indulgence only; the high sugar and total carbohydrate load per serving will significantly elevate postprandial blood glucose and should be carefully accounted for within the daily carbohydrate allowance, ideally under the guidance of a dietitian.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat content of 5.8 g per 100 g and cholesterol of 55 mg per 100 g; a 120 g serving provides approximately 7.0 g saturated fat, approaching the 7–10 g daily limit recommended for cardiovascular risk reduction.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease or elevated LDL cholesterol should limit consumption of this high-saturated-fat dessert to small, infrequent portions and balance it within a diet low in total saturated and trans fats.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chococapy Wonderland Chocolate Cake is a premium multi-layer chocolate dessert served at Kazo Premium café in Funan Mall, Singapore. The cake features alternating layers of rich chocolate mousse, moist chocolate sponge, and silky chocolate ganache, creating a complex interplay of smooth, creamy, and dense textures in each bite. It is assembled rather than baked as a single unit — the sponge layers are baked separately, then combined with chilled mousse and poured ganache before being set and sliced. The flavour profile is intensely chocolatey, with bittersweet notes from the ganache balanced by the lighter, aerated mousse and the tender crumb of the sponge. Nutritionally, the cake is energy-dense at approximately 371 kcal per 100 g, with a significant sugar load of 34 g per 100 g and moderate saturated fat at 5.8 g per 100 g. Despite a relatively low glycemic index of approximately 38 (analogous to chocolate cake with frosting per the University of Sydney GI Database), the glycemic load per standard 120 g serving is high at approximately 23.7, making it a clinically significant carbohydrate source for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome. The sodium content of approximately 299 mg per 100 g is relevant for hypertension management. The cake contains gluten, dairy, and eggs, making it unsuitable for individuals with coeliac disease, lactose intolerance, or egg allergies. It is vegetarian but not vegan or halal-certified. This dessert is positioned as a special-occasion or celebratory treat in the Singapore café dining context, typically consumed as a dessert course or afternoon tea item.

confidence_reason: Chococapy Wonderland is a proprietary café dessert sold at Kazo Premium, Funan, Singapore. No exact nutritional entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or any other primary regional source. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy NDB 18147 (chocolate cake with chocolate frosting, commercially prepared), which is the closest defensible analogue in an approved source. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database analogue for chocolate cake with frosting (GI ≈ 38). Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy NDB 18147 as the closest approved analogue; no exact HPB or ICMR-NIN entry exists for this proprietary product. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for this food category. 3. Trans fat (0.2 g per 100 g) is a minor residual value from dairy and partially hydrogenated fats in the analogue; confirmed as a plausible non-zero value per USDA data. 4. Vitamin C is 0.0 g per 100 g; this is confirmed zero per the USDA SR Legacy source, as chocolate cake contains no vitamin C-contributing ingredients. 5. GI of 38 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (chocolate cake with frosting); the high fat content of the mousse and ganache layers is expected to maintain or reduce the GI relative to the analogue. 6. Halal status is listed as false as Kazo Premium has not been confirmed as MUIS-certified halal at the time of processing. 7. The food_name has been expanded to 'Chococapy Wonderland Chocolate Cake' to provide a canonical English descriptor; the original brand name 'Chococapy Wonderland' is retained as the primary English alias. 8. Serving size of 120 g is an estimate for a standard café dessert slice; actual portion weight may vary.
